summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew so users can install it with `brew install --cask gossamer`.
summary.outcome: Created electron-builder config in app/package.json, a GitHub Actions release workflow that builds and publishes DMGs on version tags, and a Homebrew Cask template. Changes were committed and pushed to the remote branch.

repo: Gossamer is an Electron app (in app/) with a separate gossamer-serve CLI backend — packaging requires two distinct distribution strategies (Cask for the GUI, formula/npm for the CLI).
The GitHub remote is under the 'nosman' account, so the Homebrew tap would be github.com/nosman/homebrew-gossamer.
app/out/ contained pre-existing compiled build artifacts unrelated to the packaging work.

code.finding: electron-builder was added along with a build config and a dist:mac script to produce arm64 and x64 DMGs.
Release workflow triggers on v* tags and publishes DMG artifacts to GitHub Releases automatically.
Cask template with REPLACE_WITH_* SHA256 placeholders that must be updated manually after each release build.

workflow: Homebrew Cask distribution requires a separate tap repo (homebrew-<name>) distinct from the main project repo.
SHA256 hashes for the Cask must be computed manually from release artifacts and updated in the Cask file after each release.

friction: The app is unsigned (no Apple Developer certificate), so macOS Gatekeeper will block first launch — users must manually approve via System Settings → Privacy & Security.
open_items: SHA256 placeholders in homebrew/gossamer.rb need to be filled in after the first release DMGs are built.
The tap repo (github.com/nosman/homebrew-gossamer) still needs to be created and the Cask file copied into it.
Apple Developer account ($99/yr) and code-signing secrets in GitHub Actions would eliminate the Gatekeeper friction for end users.
gossamer-serve CLI distribution strategy (formula vs npm) was identified but not implemented.
